message:
feat(fp-io): SqlReadOptions::dtype per-column override (br-l9pt / fd90.11)

Closes br-frankenpandas-l9pt (fd90.11). Adds pandas-style per-column dtype override on read_sql / read_sql_query / read_sql_table options matching pd.read_sql(.., dtype={'col': 'float64'}).

- SqlReadOptions::dtype: Option<BTreeMap<String, DType>> (default None — backwards compat).
- After row materialization in sql_query_to_columns, apply_sql_dtype_overrides walks each header; for any header in the dtype map AND not also in parse_dates, casts every cell via fp_types::cast_scalar_owned. Map entries for missing columns are silently ignored (matches pandas). parse_dates wins over dtype on conflicting columns to avoid double-cast errors.
- Cast failures (e.g. Int64→Utf8 currently unsupported in fp-types) surface as IoError::Sql with diagnostic context naming the column + target dtype, NOT a panic.

Bulk-updated 19 existing struct-literal SqlReadOptions construction sites in tests to add 'dtype: None' (backwards compat).

5 new unit tests cover: int→float override succeeds + values match, missing-column entries ignored, NULL preserved across cast, parse_dates priority over dtype on conflicting columns, unsupported cast (Int64→Utf8) returns typed IoError::Sql with diagnostic message.

rch: 256 fp-io tests pass (251 prior + 5 new), cargo check --workspace --all-targets clean, cargo clippy -p fp-io --all-targets -D warnings clean, ubs Critical=0 exit 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
